title: Your youngest employees may be your most valuable AI teachers
tldr: Argues that reverse mentoring—junior staff teaching seniors AI fluency—flips traditional top-down knowledge flow. Useful frame for internal comms and change management around AI adoption.
why_fresh: reframes knowledge flow inside orgs around AI
angle: How to design reverse-mentoring programs that turn employee-generated AI knowledge into a governed internal content asset.

title: Gen Z vs millennials: content formats that work for each audience
tldr: Breaks down how two key generations actually consume content differently and which formats land. Directly feeds media-consumption habit thinking.
angle: A practical content-pillar matrix mapping format choices to generational consumption psychology.

title: Eight in ten parents say social media use has a negative impact on children
tldr: New YouGov data shows overwhelming parental concern about social media's effect on kids—a signal of shifting public sentiment that brands and platforms must navigate.
why_fresh: fresh sentiment data on media consumption
angle: What rising social-media skepticism among parents means for brand safety and human-centered content.

title: What experienced communicators wish new PR pros understood
tldr: Senior comms leaders share what's changed in PR in five years—strategy, judgment, writing, media relations. A snapshot of how the craft is evolving.
angle: The judgment gap: what AI can't teach new PR pros, and why human craft still wins.

title: Hinge's chief marketer on why community is more than a marketing channel
tldr: Hinge's CMCO explains donating millions to grassroots social groups and treating community as core, not a channel. A concrete case in human-centered brand purpose.
angle: When community isn't a funnel: lessons from brands investing in people over reach.

title: Sociable: Reddit study highlights user purchase behavior
tldr: WPP-partnered research on how Reddit users gather brand insight and make buying decisions. Useful audience-behavior data for content placement strategy.
angle: Why Reddit's research-driven purchase behavior demands a different content tone than polished social.

title: Should AI Write My Headline?
tldr: CJR's new tech advice column opens by examining what happens when you feed unpublished work into an LLM—craft, privacy and IP concerns for writers.
why_fresh: new column tackling AI craft ethics
angle: Where AI helps and hurts headline craft—and what feeding drafts to LLMs really risks.

title: FAQs for AEO: How to structure answers that rank in answer engines
tldr: With ~1 in 5 Google searches now producing AI summaries citing multiple sources, this lays out how to structure content for answer engines. Core to zero-click content strategy.
why_fresh: AEO shift with fresh Pew/Bain data
angle: Rebuilding content architecture for the zero-click, answer-engine era.

title: The best brands on social media in 2026 (so far)
tldr: First installment of Sprout's Social Futures Report analyzing standout brand posts and campaigns—a curated set of campaign breakdowns.
why_fresh: new recurring report with 2026 campaign examples
angle: Pattern-spotting: what the year's best brand social moments share at the craft level.

title: Future of the music creator economy: Tipping point
tldr: MIDiA flags a structural tipping point in the music creator economy. A signal-level read on where creator monetization is heading.
why_fresh: trend call on creator economy shift
angle: What music's creator-economy tipping point predicts for content creators broadly.

title: Content Creator Jobs: What It Takes to Work in Food, Makeup, and Gaming
tldr: Profiles the realities of professional creator work across niches as the creator economy nears $250B in 2026. Concrete look at how creator labor specializes.
why_fresh: useful overview, not a new idea
angle: How niche shapes craft: what food, beauty and gaming creators teach about content specialization.

title: You can't manage revenue. But you can manage the activities that create it
tldr: Reframes media revenue as an output of manageable activities—a process-over-outcome lens useful for editorial ops and attribution thinking.
angle: Applying activity-based management to editorial operations and content attribution.

title: Why Your Team Thinks They're Nailing PESO Model Integration (And Why They're Not)
tldr: Argues teams mistake channel coordination for true PESO integration, and lays out the shared accountability and compounding metrics that actually signal it.
angle: The integration illusion: why coordinated channels still fail without shared accountability.

title: How Philadelphia's FIFA Fan Festival Shows the Power of Print in Experiential Marketing
tldr: A campaign breakdown showing how print played a tactile role in a major live experiential activation—a counterintuitive case amid digital-everything.
angle: Why physical, tactile content cuts through in an all-digital event landscape.
